title: Request Lines, Methods, Target Forms, Versions, and Routing Inputs · Contract Triage
summary: An incident engineer receives a plausible but defective strict request-line and request-target parser; retain the raw transcript, isolate the first divergent state, and preserve a regression corpus entry. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in strict request-line and request-target parser and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Parse method, one-space delimiters, request target, HTTP version, and CRLF without accepting ambiguous extra components.
Recognize any valid method token, preserve case, then apply supported-method policy separately.
Distinguish origin-form, absolute-form, authority-form, and asterisk-form using method and recipient role.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Inspect the failure record · Debug Request Line Delimiters
steps.instruction:
debug case — A debug protocol team handles case 4527 in a fuzzing harness varying every legal input segmentation. Build deterministic Go evidence to select the request-target form without normalizing away the raw routing boundary; preserve this operating constraint: the browser model cannot open a socket or claim packet-level proof; then minimize a fuzz failure and add it to the deterministic regression corpus. Browser code models bounded byte and state decisions only. Sockets, listeners, live TLS, proxies, packet capture, fuzz campaigns, races, load, HTTP/2, HTTP/3, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Parse method, one-space delimiters, request target, HTTP version, and CRLF without accepting ambiguous extra components.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Test unknown methods, empty targets, fragments, userinfo, encoded delimiters, invalid versions, excess whitespace, and bounded lengths.

hints: For Publish the incident record · Debug Request Line Changed Case, name the types, ownership, and stop path, Request Line Changed Case precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Publish the incident record · Debug Request Line Changed Case, trace this concrete input and predict values, errors, blocking, and output before reviewing it: A debug protocol team handles case 4535 in a signed webhook receiver behind an HTTP/2 to HTTP/1.1 gateway. Build deterministic Go evidence to select the request-target form without normalizing away the raw routing boundary; preserve this operating constraint: the browser model cannot open a socket or claim packet-level proof; then lower one byte or time budget and prove rejection occurs before allocation or dispatch. Browser code models bounded byte and state decisions only. Sockets, listeners, live TLS, proxies, packet capture, fuzz campaigns, races, load, HTTP/2, HTTP/3, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Test unknown methods, empty targets, fragments, userinfo, encoded delimiters, invalid versions, excess whitespace, and bounded lengths.

For Publish the incident record · Debug Request Line Changed Case, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, failure, race, or toolchain evidence: A request line parser is complete after GET slash HTTP slash one-point-one succeeds.

checks.description: The evidence cycle runs from problem framing through changed-case validation.
checks.failureMessage: Frame the decision before modeling, and validate after obtaining a result.

checks.description: The cumulative go portfolio contains this competency’s named evidence increment.
checks.failureMessage: Add the exact evidence marker // Evidence: httpproto-request-line-changed-case-18yoaj.

checks.description: The increment includes the required executable or reviewable evidence structure.

checks.description: The evidence note explains the model, failure risk, and verification in the learner’s own words.
checks.failureMessage: Add a causal explanation using test and unknown plus specific case evidence.
